commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=886b59c5c474d528565991fcc63071f954a4eaae)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds two guards to message-level archive cron classification in `buildSessionEntry` and a regression test for a mid-transcript human `[cron:...]` message.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +36. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current `buildSessionEntry` can classify a usage-counted archive as cron-generated from user text, clear collected content, and skip later messages before memory sync indexes it. I did not run tests or a live `memory_search` query in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to choose the trusted archive cron-provenance boundary and require real behavior proof before this PR can proceed.

target.title: fix(memory-host-sdk): prevent mid-transcript [cron:...] from wiping archive content
target.body:
## Summary

When a user message starts with `[cron:` mid-conversation (e.g., `[cron:daily-digest] why does the archive vanish?`), the archive content classifier (`isGeneratedCronPromptMessage`) matches it as a cron-generated prompt and wipes ALL previously collected content. This makes earlier conversation history invisible to `memory_search`.

Fix: two additional guards on the content-based cron classification:
- `!sessionStoreAlreadyClassifiedCron` — skip message-level heuristic when the session store already has authoritative cron classification
- `!hasSeenPriorUserMessage` — only classify as cron when `[cron:` appears on the very first user message (real cron transcripts start with the cron prompt)

Mid-transcript `[cron:` matches still have their text sanitized via `sanitizeSessionText` but no longer cause cross-message data loss. Orphan cron archives (session-meta lost) where `[cron:` IS the first user message remain correctly opaque.

Addresses #98241

---

## What Problem This Solves

**Problem**: Users who paste cron log lines or type `[cron:` as part of a question mid-conversation (after prior chat) lose all earlier archive content from `memory_search`. The wipe is silent — no error, no warning.

**Why This Change Was Made**: The regex `DIRECT_CRON_PROMPT_RE` matches any user message starting with `[cron:...]`, including human-typed text. The code's own comment (session-files.ts:858-861) explicitly warns about this scenario but the original code ignored it — the wipe block fired on any matching message, not just the first. The competitive PR #98246 targets the same root cause with the same approach.

**User Impact**: Prevents silent data loss. Users who type `[cron:` prompts mid-session will keep full archive visibility in `memory_search`.

---

## Root Cause

**Trigger condition**: Any user message matching `^\[cron:[^\]]+\]\s*` in a session archive triggers content wipe at `session-files.ts:843-852`.

**Root cause analysis**:
1. `isGeneratedCronPromptMessage` checks `DIRECT_CRON_PROMPT_RE.test(text)` — returns true for ANY user message starting with `[cron:`
2. The wipe block had only `!generatedByCronRun` as guard, firing on the first match regardless of position in transcript
3. `collected`, `lineMap`, and `messageTimestampsMs` are all reset to zero
4. Once `generatedByCronRun = true`, ALL subsequent messages (including real assistant responses) are skipped

**Affected scope**: All session archive transcripts through `buildSessionEntry` with `allowArchiveContentCronClassification` enabled (`.reset.*` and `.deleted.*` archive paths).

---

## Linked context

- **Issue**: #98241
- **Related PR**: #98246 (same root cause, similar approach)
- **In scope**: Archive content cron classification wipe guard
- **Out of scope**: `isCronRunGeneratedRecord` session-meta based cron detection (separate code path, unchanged)

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Mid-transcript `[cron:...]` user messages no longer wipe previously collected archive content. Orphan cron archives (first message is `[cron:...]`) remain correctly opaque.

**Real environment tested**: Linux 4.19.112 (x86_64), Node 22, OpenClaw dev checkout

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
pnpm test packages/memory-host-sdk/src/host/session-files.test.ts
```

**After-fix evidence**:
```text
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > indexes usage-counted reset/deleted archives
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > keeps cron-run deleted archives opaque
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > keeps cron-run reset archives opaque
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > strips inbound metadata
 ✓ |unit-fast| session-files.test.ts > buildSessionEntry > skips inter-session user messages

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
```

**Observed result after the fix**: All 38 tests pass. The key test "does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript" verifies that a 4-message transcript (user → assistant → user(with [cron:...]) → assistant) preserves all content and does NOT set `generatedByCronRun`. Existing cron archive tests ("keeps cron-run deleted archives opaque", "keeps cron-run reset archives opaque") remain passing unchanged.

**What was not tested**: E2E test with a live OpenClaw gateway and `memory_search` invocation. The unit test exercises the exact `buildSessionEntry` code path that the gateway calls during memory sync.

---

## Tests and validation

### Unit tests
```text
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  38 passed (38)
```

New test: **"does not wipe archive content when [cron:...] appears mid-transcript"**
  - 4-message transcript: user("weather?") → assistant → user("[cron:daily-digest] why?") → assistant
  - Verifies ALL content preserved in `entry.content`
  - Verifies `entry.generatedByCronRun` is not set

### Existing cron archive tests (all pass unchanged)
- "keeps cron-run deleted archives opaque when the live session store entry is gone"
- "keeps cron-run reset archives opaque when session metadata preserves the cron key"
- "classifies active entries through cron parentage chains"

## Risk checklist

- [x] This change is backwards compatible
- [x] This change has been tested with existing configurations
- [ ] I have updated relevant documentation
- [ ] Breaking changes (if any) are documented in Summary

**Session-state risk**: Minimal. The change is strictly additive — extra conditions (`!sessionStoreAlreadyClassifiedCron`, `!hasSeenPriorUserMessage`) that only restrict when the cron-classification wipe can fire. Existing true cron-run archives (session-store classified, session-meta keyed, or first-message `[cron:`) are unaffected. The new behavior only prevents false positives on mid-transcript matches.

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
